message:
fix(plan): derive obligation safety from detailed flow analysis

PlanAnalysis previously checked obligation safety using only the
top-level ObligationSafety enum, ignoring the detailed ObligationFlow
data. An obligation leaf node could be marked Clean at the top level
while its flow analysis showed unresolved must_resolve paths.

Changes:
- ObligationFlow::effective_safety(): derives safety from flow data
  (leak_on_cancel non-empty → MayLeak, unresolved must_resolve → Leaked)
- NodeAnalysis::effective_obligation(): joins top-level safety with
  flow-derived safety (fail-closed composition)
- NodeAnalysis::has_obligation_issues() + is_safe() now use effective
- PlanAnalysis::obligation_issues() uses has_obligation_issues()
- SideConditionChecker::obligations_safe() uses effective_obligation()
- Race and Timeout analyzers now propagate MayLeak/MayOrphan when
  leak_on_cancel is non-empty

This closes a soundness gap where race nodes with obligation-bearing
losers appeared safe despite potential leaks on cancellation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
